page: Microsoft Azure E20_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Ev3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The instance provides 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 10 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2.0 threads per core. It is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. A 512 GB local storage volume is bundled with the instance, while no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is a bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% tier. Text processing also registers in the poor category. However, multi-core scaling is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores achieving average tier rankings. Memory read bandwidth is average at 731.15 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 50.0 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth is weak at 24.13 GB/sec.

Given its high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU and average multi-threaded performance, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high single-thread compute speeds. Favorable workloads include database operations, which scored in the average tier on PassMark, and Redis caching, achieving 11.03 million operations per second. It also supports moderate web serving and light LLM inference for small models. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the older Intel Xeon architecture offers a high volume of RAM per vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-bound applications where raw CPU clock speed is secondary to memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure E20_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs across 10 physical cores, 160.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 512 GB local storage volume. Benchmark results indicate a distinct performance tradeoff: single-core metrics, such as Geekbench and stress-ng single-core scores, fall into the bottom 10% tier, whereas multi-core benchmarks, including PassMark CPU and Geekbench multi-core, reach average levels. Memory read bandwidth is average, though large-block write bandwidth is weak. With 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads like database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation where high memory capacity is prioritized over single-threaded processing speed.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 512 GB local storage, offering average multi-core performance but weak single-core speeds.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20_v3 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 512 GB local storage on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density and bundled local storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 10 physical cores
160.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Average multi-core performance contrasted by weak single-core execution
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-bound workloads
